subject: SCIENCE
title: Sound Starts with Vibrations
objective: Relate sound to vibration and practice hearing safety.
introduction: Sound begins with vibration and travels through a medium. Loud or prolonged sound can harm hearing. You will explore examples, sort cards, practice, and answer five questions.

vocabulary.definition: A key word used in the lesson “Sound Starts with Vibrations”.
A key word used in the lesson “Sound Starts with Vibrations”.
A key word used in the lesson “Sound Starts with Vibrations”.

activities.instruction: Study the idea and listen to the narration.
activities.content: Sound begins with vibration and travels through a medium. Loud or prolonged sound can harm hearing.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: Text alternative: Sound begins with vibration and travels through a medium. Loud or prolonged sound can harm hearing.

activities.instruction: Explore each learning token, then read the examples.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: A numbered list of the same examples is available for keyboard and screen-reader use.

activities.instruction: Sort each card into “fits the lesson” or “does not fit.”

feedback.correct: Your groups follow the lesson rule.
feedback.retry: Use the explanation, not color or position.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Tap an item and then tap a labeled category; dragging is optional.

activities.instruction: What starts sound in a plucked string?

feedback.retry: Read the lesson rule again. color and shadow do not fit.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: All choices are visible text and selectable with TalkBack.

activities.instruction: Match each example to the lesson idea.

feedback.retry: Return to the explanation and compare one pair at a time.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: A list-based matching control replaces drag gestures.

activities.instruction: Complete the guided review in order.

feedback.correct: You are ready for the five-question check.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Up and down buttons provide an alternative to dragging.

items.prompt: Which statement best matches “Sound Starts with Vibrations”?

options.text: Sound begins with vibration and travels through a medium. Loud or prolonged sound can harm hearing.

items.explanation: The correct response is “Sound begins with vibration and travels through a medium. Loud or prolonged sound can harm hearing.” because it follows the lesson explanation: Sound begins with vibration and travels through a medium. Loud or prolonged sound can harm hearing.
The correct response is “plucked string vibrates” because it follows the lesson explanation: Sound begins with vibration and travels through a medium. Loud or prolonged sound can harm hearing.

items.prompt: Which choice does not follow the lesson idea?
items.explanation: The correct response is “Sound travels through perfect vacuum” because it follows the lesson explanation: Sound begins with vibration and travels through a medium. Loud or prolonged sound can harm hearing.

items.prompt: What starts sound in a plucked string?
items.explanation: The correct response is “vibration” because it follows the lesson explanation: Sound begins with vibration and travels through a medium. Loud or prolonged sound can harm hearing.

items.prompt: Which example should be used in the final review?
items.explanation: The correct response is “lower volume protects hearing” because it follows the lesson explanation: Sound begins with vibration and travels through a medium. Loud or prolonged sound can harm hearing.

assetSpecs.prompt: Friendly Grade 3 cartoon storybook concept board for Sound Starts with Vibrations; rounded shapes, warm dark-brown outlines, tropical palette, no embedded text, uncluttered small-screen layout. Compose overlays provide labels and interactive targets.
assetSpecs.contentDescription: Five connected learning tokens for exploring examples in Sound Starts with Vibrations.

safetyNotes: Use the on-screen activity only; no unsupervised physical experiment is required.
